The Law is a bionic supercomputer in the shape of a column/pillar with cubical constructs forming a halo above it and an experimental product of the "Predecessors", designated the Personality and Cognitive Synchronization (PCS) system.[1] Designed to mimic the characteristics of "angels", it was developed to reduce communcation barriers and strenghten the bonds between individuals while also maintaining thinking capabilities and personality, thus proving its effectiveness for scientific and military purposes. However, the Prdecessors weren't able to understand the PCS's true nature, in addition to the risk of disrupting the subjects' cognitive functions when used.[2] Trevor Friston the Preserver describes the PCS as "a machine lacking in self-awareness" attempting to fulfill "a mission it cannot even understand".[3] After being left dormant on Terra for a long period of time without maintenance, the PCS entered into a state of hibernation, and its operational efficiency declined drastically.[2]

Reactivating after coming into contact with the Teekaz/Sarkaz native to Terra, the PCS began to facilitate the sharing of cognition and emotions among affected individuals, and even caused a convergence of species traits, where the first Sarkaz which interacted with the PCS became the first Sankta with emotional empathy via their haloes who has since called it "the Law", although this resulted in them seen as traitors by the rest of the Sarkaz.[4][5][6]

The Law physically bounds all Sankta under its rules and has the directive to preserve the Sankta's existence, which is the origin of Laterano's thirteen rights of citizenship. Any Sankta who it decides has broken the Law's will would become "fallen".
The Law rests underneath the Lateran Basilica in Laterano, on the catacombs where the previous Popes and the Saints are interred, and its true nature is kept a closely guarded secret to all but very few individuals.

I have absolutely nothing against erotic themes in VNs and stuff it just... Really makes me uncomfortable when they're... Well, yeah.
the setting of a high school, and the age where formative experiences are core to one's personality and upbringing is likely a strong reason for why Katawa Shoujo is set at those ages.
But it's okay to not want to engage

Just trying to explain the writing aspect of why the teenage spirit can be easier to work with than the adult spirit, especially when you have someone try to make adult characters in something like *Kaiju No 8*--which I do enjoy--but then the MC feels like a teenager rather than his actual 30ish age.

I get it, but honestly you can technically make an argument that it can work regardless
Hell I think Morimens kinda makes it work well by having the setting be a university

I enjoy writing as well, and my characters in a story I'm working on currently are at 18~. But that's because I'm tackling different subject matter than the sort of experiences you'd get from a high school First Love romance, or such themes.
It's really interesting reading this as someone who is very much not the target demographic for any and all erotic stories
I honestly wouldn't really be the target audience either

But! I can't say I have some sort of personal vendetta against them 
I think good stories can absolutely include such elements, hell they can actually add onto the narrative instead of being absolute gooner slop

It also tends to affect people more when a child/teen is going through a rough situation than if an adult goes through the same experience. Plus you can get away with writing characters to be more immature when they're younger, which leads to more drama, mistakes, and naivete.
like, for example, if *Nagi No Asakura* were a cast of adults, I'd hate it even more than I already do
I'm not saying I never like Erotic stories or don't read them, but if a story has no value besides the horny within then I probably won't enjoy it
Thankfully, the nice thing about media is that there's usually something out there for anyone.
I don't think I've ever enjoyed a piece of media that was purely erotic.
If there's an actual purpose to it, it can be fire.
I have a high tolerance for eroticism overall, but I usually go for stuff that has more to it. Actual writing beyond the explicit material.
Can't say I don't get that
I haven't really played many games of the sort
But I can bear with it when the story or game is good
The best example I can give is how much I love F&H despite it all
Me neither, but that's mostly because I don't go looking.

One of the first things that gave me more of a tolerance to eroticism in general were novels, since I'd been attempting to look into adult fiction when younger. The novels I read had a grand, sweeping lore that combined many pantheons and told tales of tortured heroes.
I'd assume it's because most of the F&H stuff is framed as horror Rather than erotic
Naturally, my adolescent mind was critically struck and enraptured.

I have a pretty high tolerance due to umm

The absence of something is as much an indicator as its presence.

Leaving a blank space makes it a perfect thing for endless possibilities

Gotta be my Fourth favorite sinner

For you, the artist, the one who created the piece
That void once meant something, once was something
Perhaps it was something fond, and reminiscing it, it may seem nearly there, like something you can try and grasp but won't come to you
Something that really, REALLY impacted you once, but now, only the feeling of what it once was remains
And you can no longer remember
EXACTLY. It's a kind of modernism, almost? The absence screaming about its existence, even though it's not there.
A bit more... avant garde, I say.

Corporism is beautiful displays of the body, but it freezes, it doesn't feel.
To truly embody the emotion of a moment is hard
Yet it would make for such beautiful art
The empty space, it screams out that it was here, that something alive and moving was here.
